name: @chbrain/khai-engine-heirloom
description: khai engine content, the heirloom domain: an object kept out of exchange that carries a lineage's identity and authority across generations -- the possession you keep even while you give others away.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Heirloom as piece: an object kept out of exchange that carries a lineage's identity and authority across generations -- the possession the line keeps even while it gives everything else away.

card.wire: Attaches at Piece. The anchor (piece_heirloom.md) declares the heirloom as what it is in the room: an object deliberately withheld from exchange, carrying a lineage's identity and authority forward across generations. The law makes the kept thing legible as more than property.
card.issue: The heirloom, read three ways beneath the object. One position names who holds it (the heir, in trust, never in outright ownership); two processes name its two fates -- passed down and kept inalienable (bequest), or pulled out of the line and priced (alienation). The object is the piece; the members are what happens around it.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so a kept possession reads as load-bearing wherever it sits. The link: the persona who holds it links the heir stance and the process it is in under Projection.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real heir position or heirloom process is linked. Whether the object truly carries the line's identity is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then place the object: name what it is, who holds it in trust, and which process it is in -- passing down the line intact, or crossing out of it. Read it in the ceremony that brings it out and the account recited when it does.
